post_rendered:
Mafia Game 279
Dock, The Trading Village

  • Rules
    • Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • Do not post at night.
    • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs.
    • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • Day chat will be enabled via an AoPS private forum.
    • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
    • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • Do not imitate the mods.
  • Voting/Elimination
    • This game will partially use the voting rules philosophy of "if it looks like a vote, it's a vote". So spelling/capitalization does not matter so long as the “Vote:” part is right, and the following two rules are met.
    • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>", or c or code text: 
      Vote: <insert player here>
      .
    • Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • You may vote for no elimination to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Elimination" or: 
      Vote: No Elimination
      .
    • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though you have the ability to do so. Unvote in the format "Vote: Unvote" or: 
      Vote: Unvote
      . An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • If the deadline is met the player with the most votes is eliminated. If there is a tie, the player who received the votes first is eliminated. If No Elimination is part of the tie, No Elimination occurs.
    • Starting day 2, If more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be eliminated. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • Starting day 2, If at least half of the votes are for no elimination, then there will be no elimination. It will then be twilight.
    • The moment the deadline passes, it will be twilight and no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • Once I (or the comod) post a end-of-day scene, the person eliminated (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • Each day will be 240 hours (10 days). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at their discretion.
    • The deadline will occur the very second 240 hours has occurred.
    • The night deadline will be 48 hours after night begins.
    • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and they are not in V/LA, you will receive a PM prod.
    • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and they are not in V/LA, they will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • If a player is going to be V/LA, they must PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • Do not use inappropriate language or make personal attacks.
    • Be mature.
    • The game is to not be considered over until the mod declares it as such.
    • Don't intentionally/purposely gamethrow. If you believe you can no longer win talk to me in your role pm.
    • For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after they have run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, they may be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • I (NDMath) am the moderator of Mafia Game 279.
    • This brown (#974E03) is my mod color. Do not post in anything too similar.
    • The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules
Story
Dock was a small village in the province of pi on the west coast of an island. The small village had seemingly remained unaffected by the mafia, but that was not to say they weren't lurking about. A boat sunk not far off the coast, and the passengers managed to swim to shore, with many taking refuge in the village. The sudden influx of visitors and people trying to move in put the village of Dock into high alert.

This village has been believed to be a key target for the mafia for a while, as if they could control and stop the imports to the island it would be that much easier to control the entire island. Suspectum Industries upper management responded to the theory with something along the lines of "Yeah, sure, that makes sense. We'll get on it right away." To which they had not yet gotten on it, whatever that may mean, leaving the Dock Village to fend for itself.

With the village having the port onto and off of the island, it became known as the Trading Village, as it was the prime spot to trade gems and other goods for tools and other equipment. Someone would always trade their tool for the most valuable gem offered, although people tended to be a bit biased as to the worth of the gem in their own possession.


Setup
The setup is closed, but there is exactly 10 town and 3 mafia.
There are no vanilla players, for this game the 'vanilla' equivalent is the Trader role.
Mafia are multitasking by default.

The action resolution for this game is Reasonable Action Resolution.
Private Chats are via AoPS forum with daychat enabled.
No action may self-target unless stated otherwise in the player's role pm.
Example Town Trader Role PM wrote:
Welcome NDMath to Mafia Game 279

You are a Town Trader.

Each night, you may target a player to attempt to trade roles with them. This exchange will occur at the end of the night, after the player being traded with would have used their night action if they have one. Your trade attempt fails if you die the same night, but you can trade with a dying player (causing them to flip as your Trader role). Attempting to trade with someone who started the night as a Trader also fails. If two traders attempt to trade with the same target, only the trader with the smaller hidden priority number will succeed.

You win when all mafia members are dead.

Each Trader has a unique priority number from 1 to N, Town never has access to these priority numbers but mafia learn the priority number of Trader roles they possess and of those that die.


Alive (7)
DXtreme - Town Bodyguard
NoSignOfTheta - Town Neighborizer
Madelyn Aminecraftbear - Town Result Receiver
ChrisalonaLiverspur - Town Lazy Follower
Socoobo - Town Trader
BrilliantBanana - Mafia 3-Shot Jailkeeper
RadioActive1729 - Town Amnesiac Cop

Dead (6)
BananaBall00 - Mafia Trader
candythecorgi - Town Trader
violetmx12 - Mafia 1-Shot Bulletproof
ad333 - Town Trader
aayr - Town Trader
mathjay - Town Trader


Notable Stuff
Day 1 Start
Madelyn replaces Aminecraftbear
Day 1 Ends; Night 1 Starts
Night 1 Ends; Day 2 Begins
Day 2 Ends; Night 2 Starts
Night 2 Ends; Day 3 Begins
Day 3 Ends; Night 3 Begins
Night 3 Ends; Day 4 Begins
Game Ends!


Current state: Game Over!

post_rendered:
The Dock's biweekly paper, Doc Dock, came in with several important stories.

Boat Sinks Off Coast From Mysterious Ripples
Yesterday, a boat sank as it approached Dock's Dock. Fortunately, there were no casualties as all passengers were either able to swim to shore or get rescued by Dock's Emergency Response Team. One passenger when interviewed stated that they had seen mysterious ripples in the water before the boat started sinking, the cause of which is still being investigated. There were also numerous reports of the boat captain's incompetence, an alternative possible cause of the boat sinking.

MOST WANTED: Johnny
After crumbling one town to the ground, and burning what was left, Johnny was spotted crossing the river into the province of pi. Johnny's current whereabouts are unknown, but pi's District Leader has issued a bounty on his capture and a warning to evacuate any town where he is spotted until he is locked away. As the village of Dock is both a suspected mafia target, and had a recent influx of residents, be alert.

Cowardly Boat Captain Runs Away
The Boat Captain of the boat that sank along the coast of the village of Dock has ran way. He likely ran and avoided help to not have to deal with the consequences of his poor navigation, and potentially a license fraud. He was last seen fleeing north, but his current whereabouts are unknown. He is not a threat, but may lash out in panic if confronted.


Reminder to read the rules and setup info if you haven't already. Deadline elimination is always on, majority is enabled starting Day 2. Day 1 starts now and ends January 10th at 1:30 pm Central. You may post.

candythecorgi wrote:
every post in a game of mafia-
candythecorgi,

Every post in a game of mafia can be the birthplace for endless outcomes. A momentary argument, a day’s elimination, the entire game’s result... can all be swung completely by just one post.

A post is pure possibility. It can spell life or death for the players in the game. It can inspire emotion, both in the readers and the author. It can determine one’s lasting reputation, in and out of the game.

No matter the angle, the important part of any post is the possibility of something going right. It is the goal with each and every post for something to go right in some way; by coming to good conclusions, by communicating effectively, by bringing good attention to yourself, anything.

You wrote hundreds of posts during this game. You were faced with hundreds of opportunities to do something right. It is a testament to your dedication that you avoided all of them.

Eh? Honestly I've just been dismissing brilliant up until now. I really don't see why people want to hang them.

I agree with everything theta said in #1380 (based theta btw). As someone who was in brilliant's newbie game, I'm pretty confident that they're acting exactly the same as they were in that game. Afraid of condemning the wrong person; afraid of being judged for condemning the wrong person; afraid of even just acting scummy and being hanged. Mentions of their brain hurting and/or failing. The caps lock and everything. It would be pretty impressive if they're faking that.

The only thing that makes me pause a little bit is the slightly more risky/performative "I don't know what posts sound towny and what posts sound scummy," which I could see coming from a scumpartner helping them fake it– but it's really not large enough of a deviation from their other posts to make me actually consider them being scum d1.

And this makes me kinda go hmmmm at bb00, who was also in that game and I think should be coming to this same conclusion. Frankly the amount of sheeping bb00's done this game is getting a bit concerning lol. @bb00, can you elaborate on how brilliant's posts are too chaotic now, and why you think that's scummy?

post_rendered:
Quote:
not only does mok know nothing about me, he also knows nothing about the world at large !
I do not endorse anything that is said in this tag
There could be several reasons why someone might not know about the world at large:

1. Limited exposure: A person's lack of knowledge about the world may stem from limited exposure to diverse cultures, countries, or perspectives. If they have only been exposed to a particular community or have not traveled much, their understanding of the world might be limited.

2. Educational gaps: Insufficient education can lead to a lack of knowledge about the world. If someone has not received a comprehensive education or if their curriculum did not focus on global affairs, they may not be aware of the wider world.

3. Information bubble: Some individuals may consciously or unconsciously choose to isolate themselves in a limited information bubble. They may only consume news or media that reinforces their existing beliefs or interests, leading to a skewed or narrow understanding of the world.

4. Limited resources: Lack of access to information, such as internet connectivity or reliable news sources, can hinder someone's understanding of global affairs. In regions with limited resources, individuals may focus primarily on their immediate surroundings and have little exposure to broader world issues.

5. Language barriers: Language can be a significant barrier to understanding the world at large. If someone does not have access to translation services or materials in their native language, it becomes challenging for them to access information or news from different parts of the world.

6. Disinterest or apathy: Some individuals might simply lack interest or curiosity about global affairs. They might prioritize other areas of their life, or they could have developed a disinterest due to disillusionment, an overwhelming amount of information, or personal circumstances.

7. Propaganda or misinformation: In some cases, intentional misinformation or propaganda by authoritative entities can manipulate people's understanding of the world. Deliberate efforts to manipulate or control information can leave individuals with a distorted or erroneous perception of global events.

It is important to recognize that various factors can contribute to someone's limited knowledge about the world, and understanding these reasons can help in promoting education, broadening perspectives, and fostering a more globally aware society.

Welcome BananaBall00 to Mafia Game 279

You are a Mafia Trader.

Each night, you may target a player to attempt to trade roles with them. This exchange will occur at the end of the night, after the player being traded with would have used their night action if they have one. Your trade attempt fails if you die the same night, but you can trade with a dying player (causing them to flip as your Trader role). Attempting to trade with someone who started the night as a Trader also fails. If two traders attempt to trade with the same target, only the trader with the smaller hidden priority number will succeed.

You win when all town members are dead or nothing can prevent this.


The Trading Village was sure to be bustling with trades tonight.
Night 1 starts now and the deadline is January 12th, 2:00 pm Central.

Remi had been living in The Trading Village for two weeks now. Her job quest had led her here, as co-journalist for Doc Dock. She had thought it would be safer than where she was before. Well it was, if she had stayed there she would probably be dead by now, but Dock wasn't feeling very safe now either. Remi knew they had to get rid of the mafia in their presence quickly, they had to be here. And probably Johnny to, "did he want something with me specifically?" she feared.

When the townspeople gathered that morning, they discovered candythecorgi was dead. candythecorgi was ???
Welcome candi to Mafia Game 279

You are a Town Trader.

Each night, you may target a player to attempt to trade roles with them. This exchange will occur at the end of the night, after the player being traded with would have used their night action if they have one. Your trade attempt fails if you die the same night, but you can trade with a dying player (causing them to flip as your Trader role). Attempting to trade with someone who started the night as a Trader also fails. If two traders attempt to trade with the same target, only the trader with the smaller hidden priority number will succeed.

You win when all mafia members are dead.


For clarification, role pms received at the start of the game and traded for have the same format, so this role flip doesn't indicate if the role was the candi's original role or not.
Additionally, if you did not receive a message with a new role, that means your role stayed the same.

Dock of Dock Closed for Inspection
Suspectum Industries has issued a notice that the Dock of Dock will be closed until further notice. It has been deemed a great hazard until the source of the mysterious ripples has been determined, which have been spotted again since the boat sinking. As such, nobody will be allowed onto or off of the island for some time.

mathjay Faces Tragic Death
A villager of Dock found dead in their house, most likely cause of death being the mafia that were hiding amongst the townspeople. Their identities unknown, but it was certain that other villagers would seek vengeance for mathjay. mathjay was ???
Welcome mathjay to Mafia Game 279

You are a Town Trader.

Each night, you may target a player to attempt to trade roles with them. This exchange will occur at the end of the night, after the player being traded with would have used their night action if they have one. Your trade attempt fails if you die the same night, but you can trade with a dying player (causing them to flip as your Trader role). Attempting to trade with someone who started the night as a Trader also fails. If two traders attempt to trade with the same target, only the trader with the smaller hidden priority number will succeed.

You win when all mafia members are dead.


Elsie Discovers New Gemstone
Elsie is an archeologist and has reported to the capital that she has discovered a new gemstone, possibly worth more than any currently in Dock. It is certain that some of these gemstones will work their way to the Village of Dock soon, as all gemstones on the island tend to do.

It is now Day 4. The Day 4 deadline is February 14th at 8:15 pm Central.

post_rendered:
The day had gone like any other of the past several, at least from Remi's perspective. Yet when the ever-diminishing group had picked their next suspect to remove, something was different as she cast the majority-making vote. The suspect was far more prepared. A disguise came off and the suspect started running. The group was too stunned to react.

Remi realized she recognized this figure. It was the same figure that had terrorized the last village she was at; It was Johnny. Although alarmed at this realization, she took relief in knowing that they had successfully fought of the mafia from taking control of the Dock.

Congratulations to the Town for winning Mafia Game 279!
Role PMs
Trader
Welcome USERNAME to Mafia Game 279

You are a Town Trader.

Each night, you may target a player to attempt to trade roles with them. This exchange will occur at the end of the night, after the player being traded with would have used their night action if they have one. Your trade attempt fails if you die the same night, but you can trade with a dying player (causing them to flip as your Trader role). Attempting to trade with someone who started the night as a Trader also fails. If two traders attempt to trade with the same target, only the trader with the smaller hidden priority number will succeed.

You win when all mafia members are dead.

Amnesiac Cop
Welcome USERNAME to Mafia Game 279

You are a Town Amnesiac Cop.

Each night, you may target a player to attempt to check if they are mafia. Possible results are Town, Mafia, and 'No Result', however you will not receive this result, instead a different unknown player will.

You win when all mafia members are dead.

Result Receiver
Welcome USERNAME to Mafia Game 279

You are a Town Result Receiver.

Each night, you will receive the result of an unknown player's role. Although it is possible for you to receive 'No Result', this ability is passive and cannot be roleblocked.

You win when all mafia members are dead.

Neighborizer
Welcome USERNAME to Mafia Game 279

You are a Town Neighborizer.

Each night, you may target a player to attempt to add them to your neighborhood. (Your neighborhood will be created upon first successful action.) You are able to talk in your neighborhood anytime while alive.

You win when all mafia members are dead.

Lazy Follower
Welcome USERNAME to Mafia Game 279

You are a Town Lazy Follower.

Each night where there is more than one mafia alive, you may target a player to attempt to determine what type(s) of action(s) they used. Possible results are no action taken, a list of one or more of {Role-Investigative, Action-Investigative, Protective, Blocking, Killing, Other} including repeats, and 'No Result'.

You win when all mafia members are dead.

Bodyguard
Welcome USERNAME to Mafia Game 279

You are a Town Bodyguard.

Each night, you may target a player to attempt to protect them from one killpower, and if you directly block a kill it will be treated as though you were attacked for one killpower.

You win when all mafia members are dead.

3-Shot Jailkeeper
Welcome USERNAME to Mafia Game 279

You are a Town 3-Shot Jailkeeper.

Up to three times during the game at night, you may target a player to attempt to both protect them from attacks and block their action(s).

You win when all mafia members are dead.

1-Shot Bulletproof
Welcome USERNAME to Mafia Game 279

You are a Town 1-Shot Bulletproof.

The first time that you would die to a cause other than elimination, you will survive. You will not be aware of when or if this occurs.

You win when all mafia members are dead.


For mafia change town to mafia and mafia to town and swap colors.

The mafia team is made up of USERNAME, USERNAME, and USERNAME.

You have a factional kill that one of you may use each night alongside your personal abilities.
You may talk to your teammates anytime while alive here.
